heroTitle: Pass your German exam with confidence
heroSubtitle: Prepare for DTZ with an AI mentor. Practice exam-format tasks. Get detailed explanations and feedback in your native language.
heroNote: With good preparation, the exam becomes easier and you feel more confident.
audienceCardOneTitle: I am taking an integration course and want to pass the first time
audienceCardOneText: You may only have one free attempt, or you do not want to lose time on a retake. Prepare in a way that helps you feel confident on exam day.
audienceCardTwoTitle: I did not pass and want to retake the exam
audienceCardTwoText: A retake costs 160 EUR. Practice with exam-format tasks, prepare well, and give yourself the best chance to pass next time.
audienceCardThreeTitle: I am a teacher and want to help my students
audienceCardThreeText: Share the link with your students: they can practice at home, in their own language, and at their own pace. Contact us and we will show you how to follow their progress.
problemTitle: Courses are being cut, but the exam remains
problemIntro: This is the current reality. The DTZ exam is still there, while good preparation is becoming harder and more expensive for many people. This is the reality. But there is a way forward.
problemFactOne: Around 130,000 people are excluded from integration courses in 2026.
problemFactTwo: Some planned courses cannot start because there are not enough approved participants.
problemFactThree: A DTZ retake costs 160 EUR from January 2025.
problemFactFour: Private exam preparation courses often start at 200 EUR online and go higher.
solutionIntro: We opened free access to a large part of the platform so that DTZ preparation stays within reach, even when there are fewer courses and more expensive alternatives.
solutionStatText: For free, you get access to around 350 of the 438 learning goals defined by BAMF for the DTZ exam.
solutionComparison: For comparison, printed prep books usually include only 2 or 3 practice versions, cover far fewer learning goals, and often cost 20 to 30 EUR per book.
solutionBetaText: During the public beta, this access stays free. Good preparation should not depend on whether someone can afford another course or another book.
solutionDealText: We provide the preparation tool. You share feedback about how your exam went. We want to build the platform so more people can pass on the first attempt.
solutionIncludedItems: DTZ preparation for Horen, Lesen, and Schreiben. Sprechen is coming soon.
At least 12 practice versions, the minimum base needed for exam preparation.
Detailed explanations for every answer in your native language. If we do not support your language yet, contact us and we will try to add it.
solutionFeatureItems: Feedback and help from an AI mentor.
Advanced analytics: we estimate your exam readiness and show which topics need more attention.
Instant translation of German words and common expressions directly from the task.
Your own list of new words to review.
Available on mobile, tablet, and desktop.
Light and dark mode, plus an interface in 25+ languages.
examTitle: How the DTZ exam is structured
examIntro: The DTZ exam has four parts. Below is a clear overview of timing, structure, and what is already available on the platform.
examPartOneDetails: 4 sections, 20 tasks × 15 versions available, with AI audio.
examPartFourDetails: 3 paired tasks. Coming soon on the platform.
examLevelsText: To receive B1, you need B1 in the speaking part and at least one written part at B1.
stepsIntro: We kept the path simple: sign up, choose the exam part you want to focus on, and train at your own pace with clear feedback.
stepsCardOneText: Free, in about 30 seconds. No complicated onboarding and no unnecessary steps.
stepsCardTwoText: Start with the part that feels easier and move up when you are ready for more difficulty.
stepsCardThreeText: Get detailed answer explanations and feedback from an AI mentor as you prepare.
storyIntro: This is not a product built from a distance. We have gone through integration courses, language exams, and the pressure that comes with building a life in a new country.
storyPointOne: We are a small team of immigrants with more than 20 years of experience building software products.
storyPointTwo: We have personally been through integration courses and exams.
storyPointThree: We know what it feels like to learn the language, work, raise children, and prepare for an exam at the same time.
storyPointFour: We combined strong teaching experience with effective product development methods and built the tool we wish we had ourselves.
storyClosing: That is why we care not only about tasks and interface, but about making preparation clear, honest, accessible, and genuinely useful in real life.
pricingTitle: A limited offer during the public beta
pricingIntro: Right now, the platform is free during the public beta. We want to be honest about how this works and why this access cannot stay unlimited forever.
pricingPointOne: Free access is limited by time and by the number of participants.
pricingPointTwo: We are a small team and we pay for servers, development, and platform operations ourselves.
pricingPointThree: Paid plans will be introduced soon for people who want ongoing access to the platform.
pricingPointFour: Each subscription helps us keep free access available for people who cannot afford to pay and support more learners in passing the exam on the first try.
pricingCardText: A paid subscription is not about closing the door. It is about making the model sustainable so we can keep building the product while preserving free places for those who need them most.
pricingCtaText: Register now and we will contact you when sales begin.
previewTitle: What tasks look like on the platform
previewIntro: Below are examples of how tasks and explanations look inside the platform. This is the kind of interface learners actually use while preparing.
previewCardOneText: Audio player, timing, and listening questions in exam format.
previewCardTwoText: A reading text, answer choices, and a clear task layout.
previewCardThreeText: A writing prompt and input area to practice in the right format.
previewCardFourText: A detailed explanation in the user's language so they understand not only what is correct, but why.
teachersTitle: Use the platform for your class
teachersIntro: Do you teach an integration course? Share this link with your students so they can prepare at home, at their own pace, and in their own language.
teachersPointOne: We are not a replacement for teachers. We are a tool that helps students practice more between lessons.
teachersPointTwo: Get in touch with us. We will help you understand the platform and show you how to follow your students' progress.
faqIntro: Here are answers to common questions about the DTZ exam, preparation, and the platform.
faqExamQ1: What is the DTZ (Deutsch-Test für Zuwanderer)?
faqExamA1: The DTZ is a German exam for immigrants and participants in integration courses. Possible outcomes include B1, A2, or unter A2.
faqExamQ2: How long does it take to prepare for the DTZ exam?
faqExamA2: It depends on your current level, how regularly you study, and how much time you can invest. Starting early with the exam format usually makes preparation feel easier.
faqExamQ3: How soon can I retake the exam after failing?
faqPlatformA1: Yes. The platform is currently free during the public beta.
faqPlatformQ2: How long will free access last?
faqPlatformA2: Free access is available during the public beta. There is no fixed end date right now.
faqPlatformQ3: Do I need to register to try it?
faqPlatformA3: Yes. Registration is required so your progress, task history, and personal feedback can be saved.
faqPlatformQ4: How many practice tasks are available?
faqPlatformA4: Right now there are at least 12 practice versions and around 350 of the 438 BAMF learning goals for the DTZ exam.
faqPlatformQ5: Do you teach German from zero?
faqPlatformA5: No. The platform does not replace a full German course from scratch. It is designed to support preparation for the DTZ format and requirements.
faqPlatformQ6: What minimum level do I need to start?
faqPlatformA6: You can start before you feel fully ready for B1. It is especially useful from A2 and at the beginning of the B1 module.
faqPlatformQ7: I just started the B1 module. Can I already begin?
faqPlatformA7: Yes. That is a good time to get familiar with the exam format and increase the difficulty step by step.
faqFeaturesQ1: Which languages does the platform support?
faqFeaturesA1: The platform interface is currently available in 25+ languages.
faqFeaturesQ2: My language is missing. Can you add it?
faqFeaturesA2: Yes. Contact us, and if your language is not supported yet, we will try to add it in future updates.
faqFeaturesQ3: How can I know if I am ready for the exam?
faqFeaturesA3: The platform provides analytics on your tasks and helps you see which topics need more attention before the exam.
faqFeaturesQ4: Can I prepare on my phone?
faqFeaturesA4: Yes. The platform works on phone, tablet, and desktop.
faqTeachersQ1: I am a teacher. Can I use the platform for my class?
faqTeachersA1: Yes. The platform can be used as an additional tool for students' self-study between lessons.
faqTeachersQ2: How can I track my students' progress?
faqTeachersA2: Contact us and we will show you what options are currently available for tracking and support.
faqTeachersQ3: Does each student need to register separately?
faqTeachersA3: Yes, if each student should have their own progress, task history, and personal feedback.
faqTeachersQ4: How can I contact you about setting up a group?
faqTeachersA4: Write to us at teachers@dtz-exam.de and we will help you get a group started.
finalCtaTitle: Start preparing for DTZ — for free
finalCtaNote: Free access is limited by time and by the number of participants.
